sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 12g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 3g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for a dairy food, that is a strong sign.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 11.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin b12 at 13% daily value, vitamin a at 10% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: calcium at 21% daily value stands out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: easy protein delivery for normal diets because dairy gets real credit for convenience. clear calcium identity because that is a core category strength. slots into meals very easily because practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: not everyone tolerates dairy well because that is a real use-case limitation. fat quality varies wildly across dairy because some dairy is clean, some really is not. processing changes the category a lot because the label alone does not tell the whole story.
